lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Hello sir, are you going to make a snowman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, I'm not sure I'd be very good at it!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: I think you would be!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: I want to make a snowman, but I can't reach high enough to stack the snowballs properly.

sections.title: Talking to Buoy and Gurl before speaking to Larry again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Have you started making the snowmen yet?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: You should talk to dad if you need some help, he knows how to make snowmen!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yeah, he always used to make really big ones with us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I'll go talk to him and see what advice he has.

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: He'll say, make them really tall!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: He'll say make them shorter, because that's better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should go and speak to Larry. I'm sure he has some handy advice for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Buoy and Gurl!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Hello sir!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: How do you know our names?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a chat with your dad. He's super busy, so he's asked me to help you build a snowman!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Really? We get to make a snowman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! And I'm going to build whatever snowman you want!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yay! Can it be three snowballs tall?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: But I want it to be five tall!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Five is a bad number, it should be three tall!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: You're a bad number. Five is so much better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, wait a second. Why don't we meet in the middle. How about four snowballs tall?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Fine, it can be four tall.

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yes, four tall is okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Now, we need to pick what size snowballs we want for each tier. Why don't you take it in turns? You can do two each.

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: The bottom one should be a huge snowball!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: The next one should be big!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is working nicely, we're half way! What about the top two parts?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: The next one should be a small snowball!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: No, it should be little!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: But it needs to be small, little would look bad.

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: It wouldn't look bad. Small is better for decorations.

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: My decorations will fit on a little one.

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Why do you always ruin everything?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: You ruin everything with your silly ideas!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, wait. This clearly isn't working, and I don't want you two to fall out. It's Christmas, we shouldn't be arguing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about I make you both a snowman? It can be whatever height and size you like. One each!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: So mine can be five snowballs tall?

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: And mine can be three snowballs tall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll make you both whatever snowman you like!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you both like?

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: I'd like mine to be three snowballs tall please! And I want the bottom one to be small, the middle one big, and the top one huge!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: I'd like mine to be five snowballs tall please! I want the bottom one to be humongous, then large, then normal, then big, and then a little one for the top!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's two very different snowmen, I see why this didn't work before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do all these different sizes mean? Humongous? Normal? Little? How big are those?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: You silly billy! Humongous is the biggest snowball of course!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Normal is the normal size that you get when you make a snowball normally. It's easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, thank you for clearing that up. I'll figure something out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you two going to do for decorations?

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: I already know what I want mine to look like!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you guys find some decorations while I build the snowmen? Then you can add them once I'm done.

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Okay sir!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: We will do that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that seems sorted. I should speak to Larry again, and see if he has any advice about building the snowmen.

lines.speaker: Buoy/Gurl
lines.text: That looks good! It needs a [size] snowball next!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: They're done! They're so cool!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Yay! Can we decorate them now?

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yes! Can we decorate them now please [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course you can. Are they what you wanted?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Mine is perfect!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Mine too. It's just how I wanted it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am happy to hear that. It was harder work than I thought.

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: You did a good job. Thank you very much [sir/madam].

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yes, thank you [sir/madam]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was no problem. Merry Christmas to you both!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should go and speak to Larry. Hopefully he has that festive white wine ready for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those snowmen look amazing!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Thank you! Decorating them was quite hard!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Yeah, they took ages to do! Do you like mine?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's really good! Reminds me of something actually.

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: What about mine? Do you like it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I like yours too. They both remind me of something!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Mine is Thermy! My favourite monster! She's so big and scary, makes me go woah!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Mine is Tempy! My favourite monster! He lives in the water, a huge mass of angry wet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're both amazing! I didn't know you guys would be so good at decorating.

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Dad lets us decorate every year. We get a lot of practice!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Last year we made them into the Fragment of Seren and the King Black Dragon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What... really??? I'd like to have seen that. Those sound even more difficult than these two!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know all these monsters anyway? There is no way your dad would let you anywhere near them!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Dad reads us lots of stories, and we hear about them from passing adventurers, like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must meet some interesting and cool adventurers!

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: Not as cool as you though; none of them helped us make our snowmen!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Yes, you're the first to help us make them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you're welcome. I really enjoyed it. What are you guys going to do now you're finished?

lines.speaker: Gurl
lines.text: We're going to go and play! And maybe visit the festival. Will you be there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I sure will be. Maybe see you there!

lines.speaker: Buoy
lines.text: Bye sir, Merry Christmas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Merry Christmas, have fun!
